description_long: This is a vintage-style electric oven with a pale green enamel finish and a prominent metallic control panel at the back. The oven features four circular electric coil burners on the stovetop, each outlined in dark gray, and a set of two large oven doors below, each with wide horizontal handles. The control panel is equipped with several metallic knobs, a large black dial, and a rectangular display or timer, all arranged in a horizontal row. The oven's exterior is primarily smooth with some subtle signs of wear and minor discoloration, especially on the lower section. The handles and trim are metallic, providing a contrasting accent to the pastel body. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century kitchen appliances.
description: A vintage pale green electric oven with four coil burners, a metallic control panel, and two large oven doors with wide horizontal handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four coil burners, metallic control panel with knobs and dials, and two large oven doors with wide horizontal handles.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the oven, the side profile of the burners, and the protruding control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, flat back panel with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the side profile of the burners and the control panel's extension.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is a metallic strip running horizontally along the back of the oven, featuring several round metallic knobs, a large black dial on the left, and a rectangular display or timer in the center. The controls are arranged in a single row, with clear separation between each element. The panel has a slightly raised, boxy design and includes some labeling or markings above the controls, though specific text is not legible.
attribute_door_handle_type: The oven door handles are wide, horizontal bars mounted across the upper section of each door. They are metallic, slightly protruding, and have a rectangular cross-section, providing a sturdy grip. The handles are integrated into the door design and run nearly the full width of each door.
